title: [Bug]: Encryption envelope parser accepts non-standard JSON constants
summary: The encryption envelope parser accepts Python-specific constants such as NaN in otherwise ignored fields, allowing non-standard JSON envelopes through decryption helpers.
observed: An encrypted payload envelope with an extra NaN field can be parsed and decrypted because the extra field is ignored after parsing.
expected: Encrypted payload envelopes should reject non-standard JSON constants at parse time even when those fields are not used by the current schema.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that adds an ignored NaN field to a valid encrypted payload envelope and calls decrypt_payload.
impact: Encrypted payload envelopes must remain portable and unambiguous across databases, files, and future sinks. Non-standard JSON weakens that contract.
delivery_semantics: The fix must not change ACK ordering or sink writes. It only hardens explicit decrypt and verification tooling.
security: The fix must keep crypto material out of logs and exception messages.
acceptance: A focused regression test proves non-standard constants in encryption envelopes are rejected.
Valid encrypted payloads still decrypt successfully.
The error remains a framework serialization error.
tests: Run the focused regression test, encryption tests, payload tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md and encryption documentation to mention strict envelope parsing.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published. Include the release tag, regression evidence, verification evidence, and any remaining limitations.
